cambridge.title: The Cambridge Advantage
cambridge.subtitle: World-class qualifications recognised by leading universities worldwide.
cambridge.body: As a registered Cambridge International School, AIS Doha delivers the full Cambridge pathway—from Primary through IGCSE to A Level. Our students benefit from internationally benchmarked assessments, enquiry-led teaching, and qualifications that open doors to top universities in the UK, US, Europe, and beyond.
cambridge.imageAlt: Cambridge curriculum resources and students engaged in collaborative study

principal.title: Welcome Message
principal.subtitle: Welcome to Alashbal International School!
principal.intro: It is with great pleasure and pride that I extend a warm welcome to you all to our vibrant and dynamic learning community at Alashbal International School (AIS).
principal.body1: As Head of School, I am honoured to lead an institution that is dedicated to provide excellence in education, fostering a nurturing environment, and empowering every student to reach their full potential.
principal.body2: At AIS, we are committed to deliver a premium quality education that nurtures the intellectual intelligence, talents and skills of each student. Our rigorous academic programmes are designed to inspire curiosity, critical thinking, and a lifelong love of learning. Through innovative teaching methods and a diverse range of extracurricular activities and school events, we strive to cultivate well-rounded individuals who are prepared to thrive in an ever-changing global society.
principal.body3: Our dedicated team of educators, administrative staff and support services are passionate about creating an inclusive and supportive learning environment where every student feels valued, respected, and empowered to succeed. We believe in the power of collaboration, partnership, and community engagement, and we encourage active involvement from parents and stakeholders in shaping the educational experience of our students.
principal.name: Ms. Kathreen Agno - D'Sena, Head of School
principal.imageAlt: Ms. Kathreen Agno - D'Sena, Head of School at Alashbal International School

t1.quote: From the moment we visited AIS Doha, we felt the warmth and professionalism of the community. Our daughter has flourished academically and socially.
t1.author: Fatima Al-Kuwari

t2.quote: The Cambridge programme and dedicated teachers prepared me thoroughly for A Levels. I received offers from my top-choice universities in the UK.
t2.author: James Okonkwo

t3.quote: AIS Doha strikes the perfect balance between high academic standards and genuine care for each child. We could not have chosen a better school for our family.
t3.author: Priya and Raj Sharma

accountability.title: A - ACCOUNTABILITY

innovation.title: I — Innovation

service.title: S — Service

story.title: Our Story
story.lead: Nearly two decades of excellence in international education, rooted in Doha and connected to the world.
story.metaDescription: Discover the history of Alashbal International School—Cambridge-accredited education in Doha, Qatar since 2008.
story.paragraph1: Founded in 2008, Alashbal International School (AIS) was established with a clear vision: to provide world-class Cambridge education in the heart of Qatar's capital. What began as a small community school has grown into one of Doha's most respected international institutions, welcoming families from diverse nationalities.
story.paragraph2: Our name—Alashbal, meaning "young cubs"—reflects our belief that every child has the potential to grow into a confident, capable leader. Today, our purpose-built campus in the Old Airport Area combines modern facilities with a warm, family-centred culture, where students are challenged to excel and supported to thrive.

2008.title: School Founded
2008.description: AIS Doha opens its doors with a commitment to Cambridge international education and a multicultural learning community.

2012.title: Cambridge Registration
2012.description: Official registration as a Cambridge International School, offering Primary, IGCSE, and A Level programmes.

2018.title: Campus Expansion
2018.description: Major campus development including new science laboratories, sports facilities, and dedicated Early Years wing.

2022.title: Innovation Hub Launch
2022.description: Opening of the Innovation Hub and expanded science facilities, embedding future-ready skills across the curriculum.

2026.title: Looking Ahead
2026.description: Continuing to invest in teaching excellence, wellbeing, and pathways that prepare students for a changing world.

mission.title: Mission & Vision
mission.lead: The principles that guide every decision, lesson, and interaction at AIS Doha.
mission.metaDescription: Our mission, vision, and core values at Alashbal International School—a Cambridge school committed to excellence in Doha, Qatar.

mission.title: Mission
mission.body: ALASHBAL International School (AIS) is committed to provide premium quality education and aims to develop the skills, talents, and intellectual intelligence of students as a solid foundation for future life.

vision.title: Vision
vision.body: ALASHBAL INTERNATIONAL SCHOOL (AIS) envisions itself to be a prominent educational institution that is dedicated to nurture our brilliant YOUNG ACHIEVERS of today to be competitive global leaders of tomorrow.

accountability.title: A - ACCOUNTABILITY
accountability.body: We take ownership of our actions, uphold high standards, and hold ourselves responsible for our learning, behaviour, and impact on others.

innovation.title: I — Innovation
innovation.body: We encourage creative thinking, curiosity, and bold ideas that prepare students to shape the future with purpose.

service.title: S — Service
service.body: We cultivate compassion and responsibility, inspiring students to contribute positively to their school, community, and world.

cta.title: Join a School Built on Purpose

leadership.title: Leadership Team
leadership.lead: Experienced educators dedicated to the success and wellbeing of every AIS Doha student.
leadership.metaDescription: Meet the leadership team at Alashbal International School—experienced educators guiding Cambridge excellence in Doha, Qatar.

head.name: Dr. Sarah Mitchell
head.bio: Dr. Mitchell brings over 20 years of international school leadership experience across the UK and the Middle East. She holds a doctorate in Educational Leadership and is passionate about creating inclusive, high-performing learning communities.
head.imageAlt: Dr. Sarah Mitchell, Principal of Alashbal International School

primary.name: Ms. Emma Richardson
primary.imageAlt: Ms. Emma Richardson, Head of Primary

secondary.name: Mr. David Chen
secondary.imageAlt: Mr. David Chen, Head of Secondary

admissions.name: Ms. Layla Al-Mansouri
admissions.imageAlt: Ms. Layla Al-Mansouri, Director of Admissions

wellbeing.name: Mrs. Catherine O'Brien
wellbeing.imageAlt: Mrs. Catherine O'Brien, Head of Pastoral Care

leadership.welcome: On behalf of our entire leadership team, I warmly welcome you to Alashbal International School. We invite you to visit our campus, meet our teachers, and discover the AIS difference—a place where ambition meets belonging, and every child is known by name.

accreditations.title: Accreditations & Quality Assurance
accreditations.lead: Recognised standards that affirm the quality and integrity of an AIS Doha education.
accreditations.metaDescription: Learn about AIS Doha's Cambridge registration, Ministry of Education licensing, and commitment to international school quality standards.

cambridge.title: Cambridge Assessment International Education
cambridge.body: AIS Doha is a fully registered Cambridge International School. We deliver the Cambridge Primary, Lower Secondary, IGCSE, and A Level programmes—qualifications recognised and valued by universities and employers in more than 160 countries.

moe.title: Ministry of Education & Higher Education, Qatar
moe.body: Our school operates under the licence and oversight of the State of Qatar Ministry of Education and Higher Education, ensuring compliance with national regulations and standards for private international schools.

accreditations.quality: Beyond formal accreditation, AIS Doha maintains a culture of continuous improvement. We conduct regular self-evaluation, welcome external review, and invest in professional development so that our teaching and pastoral care remain at the highest international standard.

campus.title: Campus & Facilities
campus.lead: A purpose-built learning environment designed for discovery, collaboration, and wellbeing.
campus.metaDescription: Explore the AIS Doha campus—modern classrooms, science labs, sports facilities, and creative spaces in Doha, Qatar.
campus.heroImageAlt: Aerial view of Alashbal International School campus in Doha
campus.overview: Located in the Old Airport Area on E-ring Road, our campus spans over 40,000 square metres of thoughtfully designed indoor and outdoor spaces. From light-filled classrooms to specialist laboratories and recreational facilities, every area supports the Cambridge curriculum and the holistic development of our students.

library.title: Learning Resource Centre
library.description: A vibrant hub with 15,000+ volumes, digital resources, and quiet study zones for research and independent reading.
library.imageAlt: School library with students reading and studying

labs.title: Science & Technology Labs
labs.description: Fully equipped laboratories for biology, chemistry, physics, and computing with industry-standard equipment.
labs.imageAlt: Students conducting experiments in a modern science laboratory

sports.title: Sports Complex
sports.description: Indoor multi-purpose hall, outdoor courts, and a full-size football pitch for PE and competitive teams.
sports.imageAlt: Students playing sports on the school pitch and courts

arts.title: Creative Arts Studios
arts.description: Dedicated spaces for visual art, music, and drama with performance and exhibition areas.
arts.imageAlt: Art studio with students working on creative projects

cafeteria.title: Dining Hall
cafeteria.description: Spacious cafeteria serving nutritious, culturally diverse meals with halal options daily.
cafeteria.imageAlt: School cafeteria with students enjoying lunch

pool.title: Swimming Pool
pool.description: Temperature-controlled pool for swim lessons, squad training, and recreational use.
pool.imageAlt: School swimming pool used for lessons and training

playground.title: Outdoor Play Areas
playground.description: Age-appropriate playgrounds and shaded outdoor learning spaces for Early Years and Primary.
playground.imageAlt: Children playing in a shaded outdoor playground

auditorium.title: Auditorium
auditorium.description: 300-seat auditorium for assemblies, performances, parent events, and guest speakers.
auditorium.imageAlt: School auditorium during a student performance

gallery.g1: Students collaborating in a bright classroom
gallery.g3: Sports day on the school field

virtualTour.title: Explore Our Campus Virtually
virtualTour.description: Take a guided 360° tour of our classrooms, labs, and facilities from anywhere in the world.

hub.title: Academics
hub.lead: A coherent Cambridge pathway from first steps to university preparation—delivered by expert teachers in a supportive, multicultural environment.
hub.metaDescription: Explore AIS Doha's Cambridge academic programmes—Early Years through A Level, STEM, languages, and personalised learning support in Doha, Qatar.
hub.philosophy: We believe every learner deserves a challenging, inclusive education that develops knowledge, character, and confidence. Our enquiry-led approach connects rigorous Cambridge standards with the creativity, collaboration, and critical thinking students need to thrive in a changing world.

stem.title: STEM & Innovation
stem.description: Science fairs, coding, and design challenges that bring classroom concepts to life and inspire the next generation of innovators.

languages.title: Languages & Culture
languages.description: A multilingual community offering English-medium instruction alongside Arabic, French, and dedicated Islamic Studies in line with Qatar's cultural values.

support.title: Learning Support
support.description: Personalised pathways, EAL provision, and pastoral care ensure every student is known, supported, and empowered to reach their personal best.

cta.title: Find the Right Programme for Your Child

earlyYears.title: Early Years
earlyYears.lead: A nurturing, play-based foundation where curiosity, language, and confidence take root.
earlyYears.metaDescription: AIS Doha Early Years programme for ages 3–5—play-based Cambridge learning in a safe, multicultural setting in Doha, Qatar.
earlyYears.overview: Our Early Years setting provides a warm, stimulating environment where children learn through exploration, play, and guided enquiry. Experienced early childhood educators deliver a Cambridge-aligned curriculum that builds literacy, numeracy, social skills, and a genuine love of learning—preparing every child for a seamless transition to Primary.

0.title: Communication & Language
0.description: Rich language experiences through stories, songs, role play, and daily conversations in English and Arabic.

1.title: Literacy & Numeracy Foundations
1.description: Phonics, early reading, counting, and problem-solving through hands-on, age-appropriate activities.

2.title: Personal, Social & Emotional Development
2.description: Building independence, empathy, and resilience in a caring community where every child feels safe and valued.

3.title: Understanding the World
3.description: Science, technology, and cultural exploration through outdoor learning, experiments, and creative projects.

0.title: Foundation Stage 1 (Ages 3–4)
0.description: Gentle introduction to structured learning with emphasis on play, routines, and social development.

1.title: Foundation Stage 2 (Ages 4–5)
1.description: Building readiness for Primary through phonics, early writing, numeracy, and collaborative projects.

2.title: Balanced Daily Rhythm
2.description: A mix of guided learning, free play, outdoor time, specialist sessions, and rest periods tailored to young learners.

3.title: Specialist Enrichment
3.description: Music, physical education, library visits, and Arabic language sessions integrated throughout the week.

4.title: Primary Readiness
4.description: Structured transition activities and parent partnerships ensure a confident move into Year 1.

gallery.g1: Early Years children engaged in sensory play
gallery.g2: Outdoor learning in the shaded Early Years garden
gallery.g3: Story time in a bright Foundation Stage classroom
gallery.g4: Creative art activity with teacher guidance

primary.title: Primary
primary.lead: Strong foundations in literacy, numeracy, and enquiry through the Cambridge Primary curriculum.
primary.metaDescription: Cambridge Primary at AIS Doha for ages 5–11—rigorous, enquiry-led learning with specialist subjects and pastoral care in Doha, Qatar.
primary.overview: Primary at AIS Doha delivers the Cambridge Primary programme across Years 1–6. Students develop deep subject knowledge, critical thinking, and study habits through engaging, enquiry-based lessons. Specialist teachers in science, computing, PE, music, and languages enrich the core curriculum, while form tutors provide daily pastoral support.

0.title: English & Mathematics
0.description: Cambridge Primary frameworks building fluency, reasoning, and problem-solving from Year 1 through Year 6.

1.title: Science & Computing
1.description: Hands-on investigations, digital literacy, and coding foundations in dedicated labs and classrooms.

2.title: Humanities & Languages
2.description: History, geography, Arabic, French, and global perspectives that develop cultural awareness and communication skills.

3.title: Creative & Physical Education
3.description: Art, music, drama, and PE fostering creativity, teamwork, and healthy, active lifestyles.

0.title: Key Stage 1
0.description: Building core literacy and numeracy with increasing independence and collaborative project work.

1.title: Lower Key Stage 2
1.description: Broadening subject depth, research skills, and cross-curricular connections across the curriculum.

2.title: Upper Key Stage 2
2.description: Preparation for Lower Secondary with Cambridge Checkpoint assessments and enhanced study skills.

3.title: Cambridge Progression
3.description: Regular formative assessment and Cambridge Primary Checkpoint in Year 6 to benchmark international standards.

4.title: Enrichment & Leadership
4.description: House system, student council, clubs, and community service opportunities from Year 3 onwards.

gallery.g1: Primary students working on a collaborative maths activity
gallery.g2: Science experiment in the Primary laboratory
gallery.g3: Music lesson with instruments in the arts studio
gallery.g4: Sports day relay race on the school field

middleSchool.title: Middle School
middleSchool.lead: Broad, balanced learning that develops critical thinking, independence, and subject depth.
middleSchool.metaDescription: AIS Doha Middle School for ages 11–14—Cambridge Lower Secondary with specialist teaching, wellbeing support, and pathway guidance in Doha.
middleSchool.overview: Our Middle School bridges Primary and High School with the Cambridge Lower Secondary programme. Students in Years 7–9 explore a wide range of subjects taught by specialist teachers, developing the study skills, self-management, and intellectual curiosity needed for IGCSE success. Pastoral tutors and counsellors support wellbeing and subject choices throughout.

0.title: Core Subjects
0.description: English, mathematics, and science taught to Cambridge Lower Secondary standards with regular assessment.

1.title: Humanities & Languages
1.description: History, geography, Arabic, French, and global perspectives developing analytical and communication skills.

2.title: Creative & Technical
2.description: Art, design technology, computing, music, and drama fostering innovation and self-expression.

3.title: Wellbeing & Enrichment
3.description: PE, PSHE, leadership programmes, and extracurricular activities supporting holistic development.

0.title: Transition & Exploration
0.description: Settling into secondary routines, specialist teaching, and a broad curriculum across all subject areas.

1.title: Deepening Knowledge
1.description: Increased subject depth, extended projects, and developing independent study habits.

2.title: IGCSE Preparation
2.description: Introduction to IGCSE concepts, options guidance, and Cambridge Lower Secondary Checkpoint assessments.

3.title: Tutor & House System
3.description: Daily tutor time, house competitions, and peer mentoring fostering belonging and responsibility.

4.title: Clubs & Competitions
4.description: STEM clubs, Model UN, sports teams, and academic competitions available across Middle School.

gallery.g1: Middle School students in a geography classroom discussion
gallery.g2: Design technology workshop with student projects
gallery.g3: Debate club preparing for an inter-school competition
gallery.g4: Basketball practice in the indoor sports hall

highSchool.title: High School
highSchool.lead: Cambridge IGCSE from Grade 10 (Year 10) and A Level programmes with dedicated university and careers guidance.
highSchool.metaDescription: AIS High School—Cambridge IGCSE from Grade 10 and A Level programmes, university counselling, and leadership opportunities in Doha, Qatar.
highSchool.overview: High School at Alashbal International School prepares students for top universities worldwide through the Cambridge IGCSE programme beginning in Grade 10 (Year 10) and continuing through A Level (Years 12–13). Expert subject teachers, small class sizes, and a dedicated university counsellor support every student in achieving their academic goals and building a compelling application profile.

0.title: IGCSE Programme (Grade 10–11)
0.description: From Grade 10 (Year 10), students follow Cambridge IGCSE courses—a broad suite of subjects with flexible options across sciences, humanities, languages, and creative arts.

1.title: A Level Programme
1.description: In-depth study in three or four A Level subjects, developing the specialist knowledge universities expect.

2.title: Extended Project & Research
2.description: Independent research, extended essays, and EPQ-style projects demonstrating intellectual maturity.

3.title: University & Careers Guidance
3.description: Personalised counselling, SAT/UCAS support, internships, and alumni mentoring from Year 10 onwards.

0.title: IGCSE Year 1 (Year 10)
0.description: Cambridge IGCSE courses begin in Grade 10 with core and optional subjects, structured revision, and assessment throughout the year.

1.title: IGCSE Year 2
1.description: Completion of IGCSE syllabi, mock examinations, and Cambridge IGCSE examinations in May/June.

2.title: A Level Year 1 (AS)
2.description: Specialist A Level study begins with increasing depth, research, and university exploration.

3.title: A Level Year 2
3.description: Final A Level preparation, university applications, and Cambridge A Level examinations.

4.title: Prefects & Community
4.description: Head Boy/Girl team, peer mentoring, charity initiatives, and senior student leadership roles.

gallery.g1: A Level chemistry students in the senior science lab
gallery.g2: University fair with students meeting international recruiters
gallery.g3: Graduation ceremony on stage in the auditorium
gallery.g4: Senior students collaborating on an extended research project

cambridge.title: Cambridge Pathway
cambridge.lead: One coherent international framework from Primary through to advanced qualifications—recognised by universities in over 160 countries.
cambridge.metaDescription: Learn about the Cambridge pathway at AIS Doha—Primary, IGCSE, and A Level programmes delivered by a registered Cambridge International School in Qatar.
cambridge.overview: As a fully registered Cambridge International School, AIS Doha delivers the complete Cambridge pathway. This internationally benchmarked framework provides seamless progression from Cambridge Primary through Lower Secondary, IGCSE, and A Level—giving students qualifications that are valued by leading universities and employers worldwide.

ey.title: Early Years & Primary
ey.description: Cambridge Primary builds strong foundations in English, mathematics, and science with Checkpoint assessments in Year 6.

igcse.title: IGCSE (Years 10–11)
igcse.description: Globally recognised qualifications offering flexible subject choices and rigorous external examinations.

alevel.title: A Level (Years 12–13)
alevel.description: Advanced qualifications providing the depth of study required for competitive university entry worldwide.

b1.title: Global Recognition
b1.description: Cambridge qualifications are accepted by universities in the UK, US, Europe, Australia, and across the Middle East.

b2.title: Enquiry-Led Learning
b2.description: Students develop as independent thinkers who analyse, evaluate, and apply knowledge—not just memorise it.

b3.title: Flexible Pathways
b3.description: A wide range of IGCSE and A Level subjects allows students to pursue their strengths and career aspirations.

b4.title: Rigorous Assessment
b4.description: Externally set and marked examinations ensure standards are consistent and internationally benchmarked.

b5.title: Seamless Progression
b5.description: One coherent framework from age 5 to 18 eliminates gaps and supports smooth transitions between stages.

b6.title: Future-Ready Skills
b6.description: Critical thinking, research, communication, and collaboration are embedded across every Cambridge programme.

q1.question: Is AIS Doha a registered Cambridge school?
q1.answer: Yes. AIS Doha is fully registered with Cambridge Assessment International Education and authorised to deliver Cambridge Primary, Lower Secondary, IGCSE, and A Level programmes.

q2.question: Which universities accept Cambridge qualifications?
q2.answer: Cambridge IGCSE and A Level qualifications are recognised by universities in over 160 countries, including Oxford, Cambridge, MIT, and leading institutions across the GCC.

q3.question: When do students choose their IGCSE subjects?
q3.answer: Subject options are introduced in Year 9, with final selections confirmed at the start of Year 10 following guidance from tutors and our careers counsellor.

q4.question: How does AIS Doha support university applications?
q4.answer: Our dedicated university counsellor provides one-to-one guidance from Year 10, including UCAS, Common App, and direct application support, alongside SAT preparation and personal statement workshops.

q5.question: Are Cambridge qualifications accepted in Qatar?
q5.answer: Yes. Cambridge qualifications are widely recognised by universities in Qatar and the wider GCC, and our graduates regularly receive offers from Education City institutions and international branch campuses.

stem.title: STEM
stem.lead: Inspiring curiosity, creativity, and problem-solving through science, technology, engineering, and mathematics.
stem.metaDescription: STEM at AIS Doha—science fairs, coding, and innovation projects integrated across the Cambridge curriculum in Doha, Qatar.
stem.overview: STEM education at AIS Doha goes beyond the textbook. From Early Years exploration to A Level investigations, students engage in hands-on projects that connect classroom theory to real-world challenges. Our Innovation Hub, science laboratories, and specialist STEM teachers ensure every learner develops analytical and problem-solving skills for the future.

c1.title: AIS Science & Engineering Fair
c1.description: Annual school-wide exhibition where students present original research and design projects to judges and the community.

c2.title: Coding & Digital Innovation
c2.description: Cross-curricular coding challenges and digital projects from Primary through High School, building computational thinking and creativity.

c3.title: International Maths & Science Olympiads
c3.description: Selected students represent AIS Doha in prestigious global competitions with dedicated coaching and preparation.

gallery.g1: Students conducting a science experiment in the laboratory
gallery.g2: Chemistry experiment with safety equipment in the lab
gallery.g3: Primary children coding with classroom tablets
gallery.g4: Engineering design challenge with student prototypes
gallery.g5: Science fair presentation to visiting judges

languages.title: Languages
languages.lead: Multilingual education that celebrates diversity while building fluency in English, Arabic, and French.
languages.metaDescription: Languages at AIS Doha—English-medium Cambridge instruction with Arabic, French, and Islamic Studies programmes in Doha, Qatar.
languages.overview: As an international school in the heart of Qatar, AIS Doha nurtures linguistic and cultural competence across our community. English is the language of instruction for Cambridge programmes, while Arabic and French are taught by specialist teachers. Our Arabic and Islamic Studies provision aligns with Ministry of Education requirements and celebrates the rich heritage of our host country.

en.title: English (Language of Instruction)
en.description: Cambridge English programmes from Primary through A Level, with EAL support for students new to English-medium education.

ar.title: Arabic
ar.description: Native and non-native Arabic pathways developing reading, writing, and communication skills aligned with Qatar's cultural context.

fr.title: French
fr.description: Cambridge-aligned French from Year 3, available as an IGCSE and A Level option for students pursuing multilingual profiles.

arabicIslamic.title: Arabic & Islamic Studies
arabicIslamic.body: In accordance with Qatar Ministry of Education requirements, all Muslim students receive Arabic language and Islamic Studies instruction from specialist teachers. Our programme fosters respect for Islamic values, Quranic literacy, and an appreciation of Arab culture—complementing our international Cambridge curriculum.
arabicIslamic.imageAlt: Students in an Arabic language and Islamic Studies lesson

hub.title: Student Life
hub.lead: A vibrant community where learning extends far beyond the classroom—through clubs, sports, events, and lasting friendships.
hub.metaDescription: Student life at Alashbal International School—clubs, sports, events, and a diverse multicultural community in Doha, Qatar.
hub.overview: Life at Alashbal International School is rich, inclusive, and full of opportunity. From competitive sports teams that face schools across Qatar to creative clubs and cultural celebrations, students develop leadership, teamwork, and friendships that last a lifetime.

clubs.title: Clubs & Activities
clubs.description: Over 30 clubs spanning STEM, arts, languages, debate, and community service—available from Primary onwards.

sports.title: Sports & Athletics
sports.description: Competitive and recreational sport including football, basketball, swimming, athletics, and inter-school tournaments.

events.title: Events & Celebrations
events.description: International Day, science fairs, musical productions, charity drives, and graduation ceremonies throughout the year.

gallery.title: Photo Gallery
gallery.description: Browse moments from campus life, classrooms, sports, and special events across the AIS Doha community.

clubs.title: Clubs & Activities
clubs.lead: Over 30 clubs and activities giving every student the chance to explore passions, develop skills, and make friends.
clubs.metaDescription: Clubs and activities at AIS Doha—STEM, arts, languages, debate, and more for students from Primary to High School in Doha.

c1.name: Science Club
c1.description: Hands-on experiments, science fair projects, and enquiry-led investigations beyond the classroom.

c2.name: Coding Club
c2.description: Python, Scratch, and app development projects guided by specialist mentors.

c3.name: Model United Nations
c3.description: Debate global issues, develop diplomacy skills, and attend inter-school conferences.

c4.name: Art & Design Club
c4.description: Painting, sculpture, digital art, and portfolio preparation for creative students.

c5.name: Drama & Theatre
c5.description: Acting workshops, scriptwriting, and annual school production rehearsals.

c6.name: Music Ensemble
c6.description: Choir, band, and instrumental groups performing at school events and concerts.

c7.name: Science Club
c7.description: Experiments, science fair preparation, and visits from guest scientists.

c8.name: Debate Society
c8.description: Public speaking, argumentation, and inter-school debate tournaments.

c9.name: Eco Warriors
c9.description: Sustainability projects, recycling initiatives, and environmental awareness campaigns.

c10.name: Chess Club
c10.description: Strategy, tournaments, and coaching from beginner to advanced levels.

c11.name: French Conversation

c12.name: Community Service

cta.title: Find Your Passion at AIS Doha

sports.title: Sports & Athletics
sports.lead: Competitive sports teams that represent AIS in fixtures and tournaments against schools across Doha and Qatar.
sports.metaDescription: Sports at Alashbal International School—football, basketball, swimming, athletics, and inter-school competitions across Doha, Qatar.

s1.name: Football
s1.description: Boys' and girls' teams from Under 10 to senior level competing in QPPSL and inter-school leagues.

s2.name: Basketball
s2.description: Indoor court training and competitive fixtures for Middle and High School teams.

s3.name: Swimming
s3.description: Learn-to-swim programmes, squad training, and participation in Qatar school swim meets.

s4.name: Athletics
s4.description: Track and field training culminating in the annual AIS Sports Day and external meets.

s5.name: Volleyball
s5.description: Co-ed recreational and competitive volleyball for Middle and High School students.

s6.name: Cricket
s6.description: Popular among our South Asian community with nets, coaching, and friendly fixtures.

s7.name: Netball
s7.description: Girls' netball teams with regular training and inter-school tournaments.

s8.name: Table Tennis
s8.description: Indoor club sessions and house competitions open to all age groups.

facilities.title: World-Class Sports Facilities
facilities.body: Our campus features a full-size football pitch, indoor multi-purpose sports hall, outdoor courts, and a temperature-controlled swimming pool. AIS teams regularly compete against other international and private schools across Qatar in football, basketball, swimming, athletics, and more—building teamwork, discipline, and school pride.
facilities.imageAlt: Aerial view of the AIS Doha sports complex with pitch and courts
